obsidian.$description: Obsidian — Page canvas, primary background — the dominant surface that recedes so work thumbnails advance

graphite.$description: Graphite — Elevated surface, card thumbnail backgrounds, and content containers that need to sit one level above the page

bone.$description: Bone — Primary text, hero headlines, card titles — near-white that reads as soft rather than clinical against the dark canvas

ash.$description: Ash — Muted secondary text, subtitles, card descriptions — one step quieter than primary text for hierarchy without color

inter.$description: All body text, subtitles, card titles, UI labels, navigation — single weight (400) across every context, relying on size and color contrast rather than weight shifts for hierarchy

twklausanne.$description: Sole display face for the hero headline — a custom typeface with tight tracking and unusually compressed line-height that gives the 32px headline editorial gravitas without bold weight. The -0.04em letter-spacing is aggressive for body size but measured for display, pulling characters close enough to read as a unified mark rather than individual letters

obsidian.$description: Surface level 0: Full-bleed page canvas; everything floats on this

graphite.$description: Surface level 1: Elevated card thumbnails and content containers needing subtle separation from the canvas
